channel.title: CURIOSS Patterns — new patterns
channel.description: New patterns published in the CURIOSS Patterns catalogue.

item.title: Advertise for Open Source Interns
item.dc:creator: Bethany Philbrick
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Jeffrey Young
Will Gearty
item.description: By Bethany Philbrick, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Jeffrey Young, and Will Gearty. Reach relevant student candidates for open source internships by advertising across a variety of platforms and networks.

item.title: Assessing Students on Open Source Internship Programs
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Daniel Shown
Fang Liu
Jeffrey Young
Kendall Fortney
Sayeed Choudhury
Tom Hughes
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Daniel Shown, Fang Liu, Jeffrey Young, Kendall Fortney, Sayeed Choudhury, and Tom Hughes. Design a holistic assessment approach that accounts for the diversity of open source internship projects alongside traditional measures of academic performance.

item.title: Co-hosting Student Events
item.dc:creator: Emily Lovell
Stephanie Lieggi
Yelena Martynovskaya
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Emily Lovell, Stephanie Lieggi, Yelena Martynovskaya, Ciara Flanagan, and David Lippert. Collaborate with student clubs to co-host one-off, open source-focused campus events.

item.title: Collaborate with External Partners on Open Source Hackathons
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Create a process for ensuring that partner contributions are purposeful and that roles and responsibilities are clearly defined in advance.

item.title: Create a Donor/Sponsorship Prospectus
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Stephanie Lieggi
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, and Stephanie Lieggi. Create and publish a donor/sponsorship prospectus that articulates the opportunities, benefits and impact of financial supporting the OSPO and its activities.

item.title: Create template documents for GitHub Repositories
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Laura Langdon
Richard Littauer
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Laura Langdon, and Richard Littauer. Create template documents for project repositories as a first step to attracting contributions.

item.title: Design a Collaborative Open Source Hackathon
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Design university hackathons as collaborative learning experiences rather than as competitive events.

item.title: Embed wellbeing into Student Hackathons
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Embed physical and psychological wellbeing into student hackathons to ensure that participants can perform at their best while remaining safe and supported throughout the event.

item.title: Embedding the OSPO as Research Infrastructure
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Alex Marden
Jennifer Schopf
Michael Shensky
Michelle McDermott
Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Angela Newell, Alex Marden, Jennifer Schopf, Michael Shensky, Michelle McDermott, and Ciara Flanagan. The OSPO can be embedded as a core research facility of its university through a range of strategic, value-led actions.

item.title: Enable Student-led Hackathons
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Support student hackathon organizers by acting as institutional advocates and managing bureaucratic processes that fall outside their reach.

item.title: Engage a Hackathon Facilitator
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Engage experienced external facilitators to design and run student hackathons on behalf of the university.

item.title: Event Tabling
item.dc:creator: Nouha Elyazidi
Ciara Flanagan
item.description: By Nouha Elyazidi, and Ciara Flanagan. Learn how to run a tabling event with games, interactive open source activities and prizes to engage students across the university.

item.title: Facilitate connections at Open Source Conferences
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Sayeed Choudhury
Stephanie Lieggi
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, Sayeed Choudhury, and Stephanie Lieggi. Facilitate relationship building opportunities at open source/academic OSPO conferences by incorporating intentional introductory activities or interaction badges.

item.title: Framework for Managing University OSS
item.description:
Framework for Managing University OSS

Pattern Theme / Category

  • Awareness
  • Promoting Best Practices
  • Policy and Governance

OSPO Problem / Challenge

Often universities do not have an open source policy. How can we help them create useful policies, charters or...

item.title: Host an Open Source Conference
item.dc:creator: Allison Kittinger
Bethany Philbrick
Bill Branan
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Fang Liu
Jacek Plucinski
Jeffrey Young
Kendall Fortney
Laura Langdon
Lorena Barba
Megan Forbes
Sayeed Choudhury
Stephanie Lieggi
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Allison Kittinger, Bethany Philbrick, Bill Branan, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Fang Liu, Jacek Plucinski, Jeffrey Young, Kendall Fortney, Laura Langdon, Lorena Barba, Megan Forbes, Sayeed Choudhury, and Stephanie Lieggi. Host a conference or high profile event on the theme of open source to increase the visibility of the academic OSPO and to demonstrate its value.

item.title: Individual Consultations
item.dc:creator: David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Kendall Fortney
Tom Hughes
Will Gearty
Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Kendall Fortney, Tom Hughes, Will Gearty, and Ciara Flanagan. Offer consultations to students, researchers and faculty about their open source projects.

item.title: Industry and Community Expert Support: A Light-Touch Volunteer Model
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Daniel Shown
David Lippert
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Daniel Shown, and David Lippert. Engage experienced industry professionals and open source community experts as ‘light-touch volunteers’ to provide mentorship, guidance, and real-world perspective across OSPO programs and events.

item.title: Informal OSPO focus group sessions at Open Source Events
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Emily Lovell
Stephanie Lieggi
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Emily Lovell, and Stephanie Lieggi. Dedicate a conference session to gather feedback from academic community members about their awareness, needs and satisfaction with the OSPO’s services and initiatives.

item.title: Integrating OSS into Institutional Software Pathways
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description:
Integrating OSS into Institutional Software Pathways

Pattern Summary

Consult with researchers, faculty and staff about open source software (OSS) to create an educational resource on how the release and management of OSS fits within wider research translation, technolog...

item.title: Lower the barriers to entry for Student Hackathons
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Offer pre-event training for participants and mentors to ensure that students of all skill levels can participate meaningfully in a hackathon.

item.title: Lunch and Learn
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description:
Lunch and Learn

Pattern Theme / Category

  • Awareness & Education
  • Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
  • Policy & Governance
  • Promoting Best Practices

OSPO Problem / Challenge

OSPO is tasked with developing a response to a new funding requirement across the in...

item.title: Maintainers & Contributors Roundtable
item.dc:creator: Francesca Vera
Zach Chandler
Ciara Flanagan
item.description: By Francesca Vera, Zach Chandler, and Ciara Flanagan. Convene a community of practice consisting of students and staff who are actively maintaining or contributing to university open source software projects in the research enterprise.

item.title: Onboarding Graduate Leads for Open Source Internship Programs
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Daniel Shown
Kendall Fortney
Jeffrey Young
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Daniel Shown, Kendall Fortney, and Jeffrey Young. Establish a structured onboarding process for graduate students taking on team lead roles in open source internship programs.

item.title: Onboarding Students for Open Source Internship Programs
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Daniel Shown
Kendall Fortney
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Daniel Shown, and Kendall Fortney. Prepare incoming students with essential onboarding training that enables them to become productive participants in open source internship projects as soon as possible.

item.title: Open Project Managment Using Github Projects
item.dc:creator: Jood Alfadhel
David Lippert
Sunil Shah
item.description: By Jood Alfadhel, David Lippert, and Sunil Shah. Utilizing Github Projects to publicly and transparently organize and manage OSPO tasks.

item.title: Open Research Community Accelerator (ORCA)
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description:
Open Research Community Accelerator (ORCA)

Pattern Summary

Create a structured student pipeline for open source contributions, blending paid internships, volunteering and course credit to deliver research translation projects with real-world impact.

Pattern Theme / ...

item.title: Open Source AI Pilot
item.dc:creator: Will Gearty
Ramya Patchala
Aryan Apte
Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Will Gearty, Ramya Patchala, Aryan Apte, and Ciara Flanagan. Develop a pilot program to investigate the capabilities and use of locally run “open source” LLM for university applications.

item.title: Open Source Capstone Course
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Daniel Shown
David Lippert
Richard Littauer
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Daniel Shown, David Lippert, and Richard Littauer. Redesign the traditional computer science capstone into a professional open source development program, combining internships and layered mentorship to deliver real-world impact.

item.title: Open Source Catalog
item.description:
Open Source Catalog

Pattern Theme / Category

  • Community Building
  • OSS Discovery

OSPO Problem / Challenge

  • A lack of up to date information on how much open source software is being produced in a university.

  • There is no effective mechanism to identify or to ...

item.title: Open Source Grant Commitment Letter
item.dc:creator: David Lippert
Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By David Lippert, and Ciara Flanagan. Support faculty and research grant proposals with a commitment letter from the OSPO relating to open source and open requirements.

item.title: Open Source Software Prize
item.description:
Open Source Software Prize

OSPO Problems / Challenges

  • A lack of up to date information on how much open source software is being produced in a university.
  • A gap in awareness and/or motivation amongst development teams on best practices in open source and open scienc...

item.title: Open Source Survey
item.description:
Open Source Survey

OSPO Problems / Challenges

  • A lack of up-to-date information on how much open source software is being produced in a university.
  • A lack of a coherent campus open source community.
  • Uncertainty about what types of support might be most desired by t...

item.title: Organize an Open Source Hackathon
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Emily Lovell
Laura Langdon
Tom Hughes
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Emily Lovell, Laura Langdon, and Tom Hughes. Organize an open source hackathon to promote open source on campus.

item.title: Organize one-off workshops
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Alex Marden
David Lippert
Ciara Flanagan
item.description: By Angela Newell, Alex Marden, David Lippert, and Ciara Flanagan. Organize short, one-off workshops to educate students, researchers, staff and faculty and to provide basic information on open source and related topics.

item.title: OSPO Advisory Board
item.dc:creator: Allison Kittinger
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Allison Kittinger, Ciara Flanagan, and David Lippert. Establish an OSPO Advisory Board/Committee to build relationships with key stakeholders, enhance effectiveness, amplify impact, and maintain awareness of the wider academic and external landscape.

item.title: OSPO as Co-Principal Investigator
item.dc:creator: Bethany Philbrick
Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Bethany Philbrick, and Ciara Flanagan. Leverage OSPO staff’s open source expertise and cross-disciplinary collaboration skills to become co-Principal Investigators (co-PIs) on funding proposals.

item.title: OSPO as Lead Principal Investigator
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan. Leverage OSPO staff’s open source expertise skills to address relevant issues as lead Principal Investigators (PIs) on funding proposals.

item.title: OSPO Mailing List
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Francesca Vera
Laura Langdon
Virginia Scarlett
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Francesca Vera, Laura Langdon, and Virginia Scarlett. Create a mailing list to maintain awareness of the OSPO and to promote engagement with a broad audience of stakeholders.

item.title: OSPO Student Ambassador Program
item.dc:creator: Mia Diewald
Rosemary Pauley
Ciara Flanagan
item.description: By Mia Diewald, Rosemary Pauley, and Ciara Flanagan. Create a paid Student Ambassador program that empowers undergraduate and graduate students to promote open source engagement, events and projects across the university.

item.title: OSPO Website
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan. Establish a website for an Academic Open Source Program Office (OSPO) to communicate its mission, activities, and opportunities. The website serves as the central public interface for stakeholders within the institution and beyond.

item.title: OSS Tutorials using Authoring Tools
item.description:
OSS Tutorials using Authoring Tools

Pattern Theme / Category

  • Education and Skills
  • Tools and Infrastructure

OSPO Problem / Challenge

There is limited knowledge across a university of how to create and use open source software (OSS) effectively; and how to follow...

item.title: Piggyback onto a larger conference
item.dc:creator: Bethany Philbrick
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Fang Liu
Jacek Plucinski
Jeffrey Young
Kendall Fortney
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Bethany Philbrick, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Fang Liu, Jacek Plucinski, Jeffrey Young, and Kendall Fortney. Build the visibility of an academic OSPO by attaching typical conference activities (e.g. talks, workshops, meetups) to a larger event that addresses similar interests and themes.

item.title: Project Rolodex
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Megan Forbes
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, and Megan Forbes. Create an outreach script and contact sheet for conducting outreach with potential stakeholders during the initial stages of establishing and promoting an academic OSPO.

item.title: Publish an OSPO Newsletter
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
David Pérez-Suárez
Laura Langdon
Rosemary Pauley
Virginia Scarlett
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Angela Newell, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, David Pérez-Suárez, Laura Langdon, Rosemary Pauley, and Virginia Scarlett. Create a regular, curated newsletter to maintain awareness of and engagement with the OSPO amongst a broad audience of stakeholders.

item.title: Secure sponsorship for an Open Source Conference
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Lorena Barba
Stephanie Lieggi
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Lorena Barba, and Stephanie Lieggi. Source sponsors to fund an open source conference on campus.

item.title: Senior Leadership Keynote
item.dc:creator: Bill Branan
Ciara Flanagan
Megan Forbes
Sayeed Choudhury
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
item.description: By Bill Branan, Ciara Flanagan, Megan Forbes, and Sayeed Choudhury. Secure keynote presentations from senior university leadership at open source conferences to demonstrate institutional commitment and to elevate the profile of open source activities on campus.

item.title: Set up an informal Communication Platform
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Francesca Vera
Laura Langdon
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Francesca Vera, and Laura Langdon. Set up a a dedicated communication platform to provide students with a dedicated informal space to engage with the OSPO and with open source communities on campus.

item.title: Set up an OSPO Giving Page
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Lance Albertson
Stephanie Lieggi
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Lance Albertson, and Stephanie Lieggi. Channel direct funding to an academic OSPO by setting up a donations or giving page that aligns with the institution’s financial systems.

item.title: Source Industry Mentors for the I-Corps program
item.dc:creator: Ciara Flanagan
Stephanie Lieggi
Tom Hughes
Zach Chandler
item.category: Demonstrating value as an Academic OSPO
Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Ciara Flanagan, Stephanie Lieggi, Tom Hughes, and Zach Chandler. Use internal university connections to source industry mentors who can commit to the 40 hour mentoring program requirement needed for the NSF I-Corps training program.

item.title: Sourcing Projects for Open Source Internships
item.dc:creator: Angela Newell
Bethany Philbrick
Ciara Flanagan
David Lippert
Jeffrey Young
Will Gearty
item.category: Working with Tech Transfer / External Partners
item.description: By Angela Newell, Bethany Philbrick, Ciara Flanagan, David Lippert, Jeffrey Young, and Will Gearty. Build a sustainable pipeline of open source internship projects through active outreach to faculty, researchers, external partners and/or internal project development.
